response: Emily has 24 residents who will be divided into groups of 6, resulting in 4 groups. Each group receives 3 initial writing prompts, so the total number of initial prompts is 4 groups × 3 prompts = 12 prompts. Emily also has an additional set of 10 prompts to be shared equally among the 4 groups. Dividing 10 prompts among 4 groups means each group receives 2 prompts with 2 prompts left over. However, since the question asks for the total number of prompts Emily needs to prepare, we include all the prompts, including the extra ones. Therefore, the total number of prompts is 12 initial prompts + 10 extra prompts = 22 prompts. The library of prompts will be used by the residents to spark their creativity, and the library will also serve as a resource for future sessions. The END.
